artifacts.content:
Hi Clar,

Everyone’s finally asleep and I’m hiding in the kitchen with cold coffee, so I figured I’d actually answer your last message.

I took the water sample from our B-304 kitchen tap the other day, the same faucet where the hospice nurse from Guam Memorial Hospital showed us back in August 2023 how to rinse Dad’s chemo urinal. I can still picture her standing right here, telling us to use this sink because it was closest to his room. Ever since then I’ve been weird about that corner of the plumbing. It’s also the tap I use to fill Mateo’s sippy cups, especially since the FHP pediatrician told us in November 2023 to switch him from bottled juice to tap water for his constipation and weight. So yeah, that exact faucet is what I had them check.

I ended up drawing 1 liter for the lab and kept another 250 mL here in the condo, just in case Bella and Mateo get sick again like they did in October 2024 when Dr. Santos at Guam Memorial Hospital thought it might be from our building’s water. I labeled the little bottle and shoved it in the back of the fridge behind Tony’s hot sauce.

On top of all that, I still think about Dad’s kidney disease and how Mom moved in with us in September 2023 after he passed. Remember when her nephrologist, Dr. Javier, warned us on 10/15/23 to be extra careful about heavy metals and contaminants in the tap water? That conversation is stuck in my head every time I turn this faucet on.

Anyway, enough doom and gloom. How are your night shifts going? Text me your days off so we can plan a beach morning with the kids.

Love,
Maria

Hi Dr. Santos,

I hope you’re doing well. I wanted to follow up about Bella’s lead results and update you on what we’re doing at home.

Ever since Isabella’s bloodwork at Guam Memorial Hospital in early February 2025 showed mild lead exposure, I haven’t been able to look at our kitchen tap in Unit B-304 at Oceanside Villas the same way. That’s the same cold-water faucet I’ve been using for her water bottle and for Mateo’s sippy cups, and it’s right by the fridge where Tony keeps his insulin pens in the door since his Type 2 diabetes diagnosis back in March 2022. I use that tap to mix his sugar-free drink packets too, so I’m worried about all three of them.

I collected 1 L from that kitchen tap and took it to the UOG WERI water lab, and I also drew an extra 250 mL that I kept here in the condo in case we need to compare anything later. We’ve switched to bottled water for drinking and cooking until we get results. I’m also more anxious because when Bella and Mateo both had that bad stomach bug in October 2024, you mentioned the possibility that something in our building’s water could be contributing.

Once I receive the report, I’ll email you a copy so we can decide if we should re-test Bella’s blood or take any other steps. If there’s anything else you recommend we do in the meantime (filters, specific brands of bottled water, etc.), please let me know.

Thank you again for looking out for her.

Best,
Maria Lourdes Cruz
Unit B-304, Oceanside Villas

metadata.subject: Bella’s lead exposure and our kitchen tap

artifacts.content:
Hi Jon,

You’re going to roll your eyes, but I just paid another lab bill out of my own pocket. On February 27, 2025 I put $285 on my personal Bank of Guam checking account (the one ending in 4317) for the new round of tests, because the Oceanside Villas HOA is still 60 days late reimbursing me for the last water testing invoices. If they stiff me again, I’m seriously filing in small claims.

I’m not doing this just for fun. We’ve had residents in Building B complaining of stomach issues since around November 2024, and the family in Unit B-306 had their little one treated for a severe E. coli infection at Guam Memorial Hospital in December 2024. That scared everyone. I already talked to Atty. Paul Sablan about it in case this turns into something bigger.

At the same time, Pacific Horizons is under that 90-day corrective action plan with Guam EPA that started January 2, 2025 because of the prior noncompliance at the Dededo building. So I need good, clean results for Oceanside Villas to show we’re actually fixing things. It feels like I’m stuck between residents, the HOA, and GEPA, and I’m the only one actually paying for anything.

Mom keeps telling me to calm down and not let it raise my blood pressure like Dad, but I’m tired of being the responsible one while everyone else drags their feet.

Anyway, enough ranting. Are you still free Sunday after Mass to help Tony barbecue at Mom’s? The kids have been asking when Uncle Jon is coming over.

Love,
Ate

Personal note – water worries + family health

I need to get all of this out of my head in one place.

Dad: Ramon’s kidney disease and his death at Guam Regional Medical City in July 2023 still sit heavy. After Mom moved into B-304 in September 2023, her nephrologist, Dr. Javier, warned us on 10/15/2023 to be very careful about heavy metals and contaminants in our tap water. That’s when I started side-eyeing every glass from the kitchen sink.

Bella: early February 2025, Dr. Santos at Guam Memorial Hospital told us her blood tests showed mild lead exposure. I keep replaying all the times I filled her bottle from that same kitchen cold-water tap in Unit B-304. Before that, in October 2024, both Bella and Mateo had that awful GI episode, and Dr. Santos mentioned our building’s water as a possible factor.

Mateo: since the FHP Health Center pediatrician told us in November 2023 to switch him from bottled juice to tap water for constipation and weight, I’ve been using that faucet for his daily sippy cups. Now I feel guilty about following the advice.

Tony: diagnosed with Type 2 diabetes in March 2022; his insulin pens live in the fridge door right next to that sink. I use the same tap to mix his sugar-free drinks. Lately his skin rashes have flared again, and his dermatologist at Island Skin Clinic keeps asking if anything changed at home.

Action items: keep using bottled water, follow up with WERI results, ask all their doctors directly about long-term exposure risk, and seriously push the HOA and Pacific Horizons to budget for new plumbing in Building B.

Hi Paul,

Quick update from my end before your meeting this week.

We’ve continued to get complaints from residents at Oceanside Villas, especially in Building B. Several families have mentioned gastrointestinal issues since around November 2024. The most serious was the family in Unit B-306, whose child was treated for a severe E. coli infection at Guam Memorial Hospital in December 2024. Parents keep asking me whether the water is safe, and honestly, I don’t know what to tell them beyond “we’re testing.”

As you know, Pacific Horizons Property Management is already under that 90-day corrective action plan with Guam EPA starting January 2, 2025, because of the prior noncompliance at the Dededo property. I’m trying to show good faith by getting current data for Oceanside Villas. For the latest round, I collected 1 L from my own kitchen cold-water tap in Unit B-304 and kept an extra 250 mL here as retained volume in case any tenants file more complaints.

I’m also personally worried because my daughter Isabella was diagnosed with mild lead exposure by Dr. Santos at Guam Memorial Hospital in early February 2025, and that’s the same faucet we use for drinking water. Between that and Mom’s nephrologist, Dr. Javier, warning us back on 10/15/2023 to be careful about heavy metals in tap water after Dad’s kidney issues, I feel like this is more than just a “PR problem.”

Once the lab sends results, I’ll forward everything so you can advise whether we’re looking at potential liability, especially regarding B-306.

Thanks,
Maria L. Cruz
Property Manager, Pacific Horizons

metadata.subject: Oceanside Villas – resident illnesses and compliance status

artifacts.content:
Event: Collect and deliver water samples – B-304 kitchen tap

Date: Friday, 2026-02-28
Time: 8:30 a.m. – 12:00 p.m.
Location: Start at Unit B-304, Oceanside Villas → drive to UOG WERI Water Quality Lab

Details:

– 8:30 a.m.: Clear dishes from B-304 kitchen sink, remove faucet aerator.
– 8:40 a.m.: Run cold-water tap ~5 minutes until temp and color stabilize. This is the same tap over the cabinet where I keep the bleach and roach spray from the minor cockroach infestation in Building B back in September 2023, and the one I used on May 12, 2024 to rinse the mop bucket after cleaning the sewage backup by our unit door near the leaking main line. Also the faucet closest to Building B’s main cold-water riser.
– 8:50 a.m.: Fill 1 L primary bottle + 250 mL extra from that tap. Remember: kids’ GI issues in October 2024, Bella’s mild lead exposure early Feb 2025 per Dr. Santos at GMH, Mom’s kidney concerns and Dr. Javier’s 10/15/2023 warning about heavy metals.
– 9:15 a.m.: Label bottles, pack in cooler with ice packs; keep in my custody.
– 10:30 a.m.: Drive from Oceanside Villas to UOG WERI, drop off samples, get itemized receipt (for HOA reimbursement / possible small-claims if they delay again).
– 12:00 p.m.: Back to office at Pacific Horizons; email Daniel and file receipt.

Reminder: pick up Mateo from daycare by 3:30 p.m. and Bella from school at 4:00 p.m.

[Group chat: “Cruz Sibs + Mom”]

Maria: Just got home from GEPA’s follow-up visit, I’m wiped.

Clarissa: How bad was it?

Maria: Not terrible, but Mr. Santos from the GEPA Safe Drinking Water Program was here forever. He had me pull 1 L from the B-304 bathroom tap and another 250 mL to keep as retained volume, like he suggested on Feb 3, 2025 when he came the first time about the tenant complaints.

Jon: More water drama 😑

Maria: Always. I reminded him Pacific Horizons is still under that 90-day corrective action plan with Guam EPA that started Jan 2, 2025 because of the Dededo mess. He just nodded and said we need to “document everything” if Oceanside Villas residents keep getting sick.

Clarissa: Speaking of sick, how’s Bella after the mild lead exposure thing?

Maria: Still stressed about it. Early Feb 2025 at GMH with Dr. Santos is burned into my brain. That, plus Dad’s kidney disease and Mom’s nephrologist Dr. Javier warning us on 10/15/23 about heavy metals in the tap, and the B-306 kid’s E. coli hospitalization in Dec 2024… it’s a lot.

Mom: Don’t worry too much, just keep using bottled water for the kids and Tony.

Maria: I am. Especially since Tony’s diabetes and skin rashes are acting up again. I keep thinking about that Legionella scare at Oceanside Villas last March 2024 too. I feel like our whole building is a walking case study.

Jon: Ok, but are we still doing BBQ Sunday? I’ll bring ribs.

Maria: Yes, BBQ is non-negotiable 😂 I’ll handle kelaguen and the kids.

From: Maria Lourdes Cruz <maria.cruz@pacifichorizonspm.com>
To: Daniel Reyes <daniel.reyes@pacifichorizonspm.com>
Cc: Maintenance <maintenance@pacifichorizonspm.com>
Date: 2025-01-16
Subject: Follow-up on Oceanside Villas Building B water sampling

Hi Daniel,

Quick update on the water sample I pulled yesterday from Oceanside Villas, Building B. For the WERI submission I collected 1 L from the laundry room faucet in the Oceanside Villas Condominium Complex laundry room, per their instructions for first-draw testing. While I was there, I also drew an extra 250 mL that I didn’t submit because Pacific Horizons Property Management, LLC is understandably worried about potential liability after the Legionella scare we had at Oceanside Villas in March 2024.

Given the history with that Legionella scare and the ongoing complaints about yellowish tint and metallic taste from a few of the residents (including my own unit B-304), I thought it was prudent to retain a small volume in case there are questions later about our response or any employer liability concern. Right now I’ve labeled and stored the retained 250 mL temporarily at the office so we can decide whether to keep it longer term, discard it, or have it tested independently if WERI’s results raise any red flags.

Please let me know if you want me to document this in the incident log for Oceanside Villas or notify the HOA board formally. I’m also happy to loop in our contact at Guam Waterworks Authority once WERI sends back the report.

Thanks,
Maria

—
Maria Lourdes Cruz
Property Manager
Pacific Horizons Property Management, LLC
Marine Corps Drive, Tamuning, GU

metadata.subject: Follow-up on Oceanside Villas Building B water sampling

artifacts.content:
To: Clarissa 👩‍🦰
Date: 2025-01-15, 8:42 PM

Clar,

Just got home and finally sitting down after dealing with water stuff all day. I wanted to let you know what I ended up doing in case this turns into a bigger mess later.

I pulled 1 L for the WERI lab like they asked, and another 250 mL that I stored in mom Lourdes’ fridge in Mangilao this afternoon (15 January 2025). I labeled the bottle with “Oceanside Villas – Unit B-304 kitchen tap” and the time I collected it. I figured if Tony’s rash keeps flaring and his dermatologist at Island Skin Clinic, Tamuning, starts asking questions, we’ll at least have something to point to as proof that it might be the water at our Oceanside Villas unit.

Tony’s still getting those weird patches on his arms and neck, and the dermatologist thinks it could be contact-related, maybe from our home water. Between that and the kids using the same tap for brushing teeth and filling their water bottles, I just don’t want to take chances. If anything gets worse, we can tell the doctor we’ve got a stored sample from today that matches what we’re using at home.

Anyway, if mom asks why there’s a mystery bottle in her fridge, please remind her it’s mine and not to toss it. I told her already, but you know how she is when she’s cleaning.

Love you,
Maria

Title: Oceanside Villas water sampling + Tony rash documentation
Date: 2025-01-15
Time: 9:00 AM – 11:00 AM
Location: Oceanside Villas Condominium Complex (Unit B-304 kitchen + Building B laundry room) / Mom’s house in Mangilao, GU

Description / Notes:

- Collect water samples from our unit and common area for WERI.
- Volume: Total volume I drew was 1 L for the lab and another 250 mL that I stored in my mom Lourdes’ fridge in Mangilao on 15 January 2025, in case we need proof later that the water at our Oceanside Villas unit was causing the rashes my husband Tony’s dermatologist at Island Skin Clinic has been treating.
- Label extra 250 mL clearly with date/time, “Unit B-304 tap,” and keep it in back of mom’s fridge so it doesn’t get thrown out.
- For WERI: 1 L collected from the laundry room faucet in the Oceanside Villas Condominium Complex laundry room (Building B) following their instructions.
- Also drew an extra 250 mL on site that I did NOT submit because Pacific Horizons Property Management, LLC is worried about potential liability after the Legionella scare we had at Oceanside Villas in March 2024; retaining that volume separately for internal records in case of any employer liability concern.
- After samples are dropped off, email Daniel at Pacific Horizons with details and log complaints from residents about yellowish/metallic-tasting water.

Reminders:
- Ask Tony tonight how his skin is doing and note any changes before his next follow-up at Island Skin Clinic.
- Call mom (Lourdes P. Cruz) to remind her not to throw away the labeled bottle.
